pattern: A sweep-line over segment endpoints accumulates color-sum deltas; every endpoint is a real boundary, so each gap with positive sum becomes its own output piece.
hints: Think of each painted segment as adding its color at the start point and removing it at the end point.
Record a +color delta at every start and a -color delta at every end, then walk the sorted distinct coordinates accumulating the current mixed sum.

Between two adjacent coordinates the mixed sum is constant; emit a segment for each gap with positive sum. Do not merge neighbors with equal sums — distinct endpoints mark a change in the actual color set.

edge_inputs.description: three overlapping segments mix in two regions
gap and partial overlaps create four pieces
equal sums on different regions must stay separate
two identical-range segments combine into one sum
adjacent touching segments do not overlap
nested segment produces three regions, outer ones share endpoints

stress.description: many random segments over a compact coordinate range with distinct colors
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    colors = rng.sample(range(1, 1000000000), n)
    segs = []
    for i in range(n):
        a = rng.randrange(1, 100000)
        b = rng.randrange(a + 1, 100001)
        segs.append([a, b, colors[i]])
    return [segs]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def splitPainting(self, segments):
        from collections import defaultdict
        diff = defaultdict(int)
        for start, end, color in segments:
            diff[start] += color
            diff[end] -= color
        points = sorted(diff.keys())
        result = []
        running = 0
        prev = None
        for p in points:
            if prev is not None and running > 0:
                result.append([prev, p, running])
            running += diff[p]
            prev = p
        return result
